key_insight: Polymer glass transition temperature (Tg) is predicted well by chemistry and molecular topology alone for most high-Tg polymers, while processing effects matter mainly for specific outliers.
engineering_relevance: For polymer design and screening, topological descriptors offer a strong first-pass Tg predictor, but processing history should be considered for materials with strong intermolecular interactions or sensitive fabrication routes.
method: Built a machine-learning model using polymer topological descriptors to predict Tg.
Extended evaluation to a larger polymer set.
Compared chemistry-only features against an expanded feature set including processing parameters.
evidence_signal: The chemistry-based model maintained consistent predictive performance across most polymers, with notable prediction gaps appearing for solution-cast and high-temperature-annealed samples.
watch_item: Processing-dependent effects can cause significant Tg deviations in certain polymer classes, so chemistry-only predictions may miss important outliers.
paper_title: Informatics Modeling of High Tg Polymers: Assessing the Role of Processing versus Chemistry
